description: RareDx AI proposes an open-source AI workflow for rare disease diagnosis support. The project claims it can help patient advocacy groups and clinicians identify candidate rare diseases from symptoms, literature, and public biomedical databases.
claims: The system can map patient symptom descriptions to candidate rare diseases.
The model can cite biomedical literature or public disease databases for each suggestion.
The team will release an open-source prototype and evaluation dataset.
milestones: Collect and document a small benchmark dataset.
Publish a public demo and GitHub repository.

previous_rounds.stated_goal: Create initial rare disease diagnosis workflow.
previous_rounds.reported_progress: Prototype design and literature review completed.

review_notes: This is a sample project for testing GLM-5.1 review workflow. Replace it with one of the 49 real Spark DeSci projects.
